mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate corporate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in more than 60 percent of co-occurring expansion periods, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration yields positive 20-day returns in late-cycle periods, or the predictive power vanishes when the business cycle indicator exceeds the 75th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent signals extreme monetary accommodation that fuels speculative equity positioning, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 65 percent of observations, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, surges in housing starts reflect renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Housing start increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns in recovery regimes, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 when applied to post-recession data windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum that boosts short-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates with negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the rank correlation coefficient remains below 0.01 across rolling 1-year windows.
